title: commitPairGit leaves the dossier pair STAGED when the commit permanently fails — polluting the next cycle's tree-diff/clean-tree checks
summary: internal/dossier/write.go commitPairGit stages cycle-N.{json,md} then commits with bounded retry. On a PERMANENT failure (e.g. no committer identity — the exact mode that turned TestOrchestrator_TriageLeakRecover red on ubuntu runners) it returns an error but leaves both files staged. The best-effort caller (RunCycle) only WARNs, so the staged pair silently survives into the next cycle where the tree-diff guard / clean-tree checks attribute it to the wrong actor.
root_cause: The stage->commit sequence has no rollback arm: failure exits with the index half-mutated, violating the guard-friendly invariant that a failed best-effort side effect leaves the tree exactly as found.
fix: On giving up (permanent error or retry exhaustion), best-effort 'git reset -q -- cycle-N.json cycle-N.md' scoped to the pair, then return the original error (reset failure appended, never masking). Regression test: repo with commit forced to fail (identity-less env via GIT_CONFIG env override) -> commitPair returns error AND git status --porcelain is empty afterwards.
acceptance: Failed dossier commit leaves zero staged paths (regression test asserts clean porcelain).
Original commit error is still returned (reset never masks it).
go vet, -race on internal/dossier, apicover -enforce green.
operator_note: VERIFIED ALREADY FIXED 2026-07-14 (landed autonomous cycle 6c5355ee): commitPairGit calls unstagePair (git reset) on BOTH the permanent-error and retry-exhausted paths; rollback_test.go forces a permanent commit failure and asserts the index is clean; `go test -race ./internal/dossier` GREEN. Stale item — closing, no reimplementation.
